title: vaccinium vitis-idaea subsp. merrillianum
intro: Vaccinium vitis-idaea subsp. merrillianum, also known as the dwarf cranberry or mountain cranberry, is a subspecies of the cranberry plant that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Vaccinium vitis-idaea subsp. merrillianum include reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
